title: The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels
content:
{{Top|rwm|ref|imp|reb|italics=1}} {{ReferenceBook |image=[[File:NEGVV.jpg]] |title=''The New Essential Guide
to Vehicles and Vessels'' |author=[[W. Haden Blackman]] |cover artist=[[Steven D. Anderson]] |illustrator=[[Ian Fullwood]] |editor=*[[Allan Kausch]] {{C|[[Lucasfilm]]}} *[[Steve Saffel]] {{C|[[Del Rey]]}} |publisher=[[Del Rey]] |release date=[[September 30]], [[2003]]{{RandomHouseOld|url=catalog/display.pperl?isbn=9780345449023|text=Star Wars: The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels}} |type=Paperback |pages= |dimensions= |weight= |isbn=9780345449023 |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} The ninth book in [[Del Rey]]'s [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|Essential Guide]] series, '''''The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels''''' features an in-depth look at one hundred ships, fighters, and other flying machines from the [[original trilogy]], [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']], [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']], as well as the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], including the [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''New Jedi Order'']] series, each with its own one- to two-page spread including detailed schematics. There were, however, some glaring errors throughout the book. It was reprinted as part of ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters, Weapons and Technology, and Vehicles and Vessels]]'' in [[2005]]. ==Contents== *Introduction *A Layperson's Guide to Technology *Major Manufacturers *[[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]] *[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin's]] [[XJ-6 airspeeder/Legends|Airspeeder]] *[[Airspeeder/Legends|Airspeeder]] ***[[Yorik-stronha]] (''[[Stalking Moon]]'') ***[[Yorik-ta]] ***''[[Zoomer]]'' **[[Hyperdrive/Legends|Hyperdrive]] Classification ==Continuity== *An MTT is referred to as an AAT. *''Home One''{{'s}} illustration is the incorrect type of Mon Cal cruiser. *On the ''Home One'' schematics, "Turbolaser Pod" is "2" whilst "Sensor Array" is "3," but the schematics show "Turbolaser Pod" as being "3" instead of "2." *AT-ATs are listed at 15 meters tall. *The ''Executor'' is listed at 12,800 meters long. *Because the book was published prior to the release of ''Revenge of the Sith'', fighter acceleration is scaled without consideration of ''Revenge of the Sith''–era fighters such as the Eta 2 ''Actis'', V-wing or ARC-170 that they were designed to replace and have inexplicably lower performance statistics in spite of sources such as ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' stating that TIE Fighters could outperform V-wings and Eta 2s, as well as Vader flying the TIE Advanced x1 in ''A New Hope'' rather than an Eta 2. *The affiliation for the A-wing is listed as Galactic Empire instead of Rebel Alliance. *Guri is claimed to be a cyborg when she is a human replica droid. *The [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] is called the "final battle" of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], but the war actually ended [[19 ABY/Legends|15 years]] after Endor upon the conclusion of the [[Battle of Yaga Minor (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Yaga Minor]]. *[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Roos Tarpals/Legends|Tarpals]]' "ouch time" line is used as the quote for the AAT, despite the line actually being in response to the activation of the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] ranks. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from The New
